BEEN TRADING and importantly studying for 20 years....the one glaring defect I notice with all ORB techniques is that traders using this, ignore the past as if it is irrelevant...it is not...Take a look at the time  8 ; 50...prior to the open there is a high made and it is almost on the high you point out on the ORB...And that high is a beautiful evening star and it forms a double top with the prior price action, 75 minutes prior. This constitutes a double top entry and yea, I would have shorted as the  five minute large red bar is forming at about four minutes into that five minute bar, and my stop above the evening star...NO NEED TO WAIT FOR THE ORB BREAKOUT....I LOVE your manner of cutting your losses employing the one minute bar. As far as the ORB, your approach is great....the best technique I have ever run across....The fact you wait for 15 minutes and not a five minute ORB is correct.
Does it work on Forex pair?

As a newbie about to invest, you must have these four things in mind 
1. Have a long term mindset.
2. Be willing to take risk.
3. Be careful on money usage, if you're not spending to earn back, then stop spending.
4. Never claim to know - Ask questions and it's best you work with a financial advisor

1. Win more than you lose.  
2. Use filters.  
3. Consider probabilities, not certainties.  
4. Take breaks instead of quitting.  
5. Develop a strategy that matches your personality.  
6. Avoid chasing losses.  
7. Focus on self-improvement.  
8. Understand the bigger picture.  
9. Seek help when needed.  
10. Prioritize good sleep.
